resources.address: Local Public Housing Authority offices nationwide
resources.description: Federal rental assistance program administered by local Public Housing Authorities. Eligible families pay about 30 percent of income toward rent; the voucher covers the rest, up to a payment standard. Waitlists vary by city, sometimes years long.

eligibility.exceptions: Non-citizens with eligible immigration status (LPR, refugee, asylee) qualify. Mixed-status households receive prorated assistance.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Very low income (under 50% AMI), age 18+, citizen or eligible immigrant, no recent drug-related or violent crime convictions.

form.form_name: HUD-50058 Family Report and PHA application
required_documents: Social Security cards for all household members
Proof of income (pay stubs, benefit letters)
Proof of citizenship or immigration status

resources.description: HUD-funded emergency shelters providing immediate overnight stay, meals, and case management. Call 211 to be routed to the nearest open bed. Some shelters are family-only; others serve single adults.

eligibility.exceptions: Sober shelters require active sobriety; LGBTQ+ youth shelters serve ages 14-24 only; DV shelters serve survivors of intimate partner violence regardless of gender.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone experiencing literal homelessness tonight; no income or documentation requirement.

required_documents: Photo ID if available, but not required for entry

resources.description: Short-term (3-24 month) rental assistance plus housing search help and case management for people moving out of homelessness into permanent housing. Pays security deposit, first months of rent, and utility startup costs.

eligibility.exceptions: Some RRH providers prioritize veterans, families with children, or chronically homeless individuals based on local CoC priorities.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Literally homeless per HUD definition; very low income; able to sustain rent after assistance ends.

resources.agency_name: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
resources.description: Federal program that helps low-income households pay heating and cooling bills, weatherize their homes, and handle utility crises (shutoff notices). Funded annually; apply early in the season.

eligibility.exceptions: At least one household member must be a US citizen or qualified non-citizen; mixed-status households are eligible.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Household income at or below 150 percent of federal poverty level (varies by state). Responsible for home energy costs.

required_documents: Proof of income for last 30 days

resources.description: Federally subsidized rental units owned by local Public Housing Authorities. Rent is set at approximately 30 percent of adjusted household income. Waitlists are typically long; some PHAs maintain preference categories for veterans, elderly, or working families.

eligibility.exceptions: Income limits vary by city (low income = 80% AMI). Non-citizens with eligible immigration status qualify; mixed-status households get prorated assistance.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Low income (under 80% AMI); citizen or eligible immigrant; pass criminal background check (no recent violent or drug-related felonies).

resources.agency_name: National Domestic Violence Hotline Shelter Referral
resources.description: 24/7 hotline that connects survivors of intimate partner violence to confidential emergency shelter, safety planning, and longer-term housing programs. Calls are free and not traced. Shelter location is kept confidential from abusers.

eligibility.exceptions: Most shelters accept survivors regardless of gender, citizenship status, or substance use history. Pets may need separate arrangements via Safe Haven for Pets program.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone fleeing or experiencing intimate partner violence. No income, ID, or residency requirement.

resources.agency_name: Continuum of Care Permanent Supportive Housing
resources.description: Long-term rental subsidy combined with voluntary supportive services (case management, mental health, substance use treatment) for people with disabilities who have been chronically homeless. Subsidy continues as long as the household needs it.

eligibility.exceptions: Chronically homeless is defined as homeless for 12 months continuously or 4 episodes in 3 years, with a documented disabling condition.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Chronically homeless individual or family with a disabling condition; extremely low income.

resources.description: Joint HUD-VA program combining a Housing Choice Voucher with VA case management and clinical services for homeless veterans. Active outreach; relatively short wait compared to general Section 8.

eligibility.exceptions: Other-than-honorable discharges may still qualify; VA evaluates on a case-by-case basis.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Homeless veteran eligible for VA healthcare; very low income; case-management willing.

resources.description: Transitional housing programs (typically 6-24 months) with case management, employment support, and life skills training. Sites operate under local Corps; rules vary (some sober-living, some family-only).

eligibility.exceptions: Faith-based but does not require religious participation. Some sites are sober-only; others are recovery-friendly.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Homeless or unstably housed; willing to engage with case management and employment program; sober if site requires.

resources.description: One-time emergency rental and utility assistance to prevent eviction or restore utilities, typically up to 1-3 months of past-due rent. Pairs with budget counseling. Funds are limited and refresh quarterly.

eligibility.exceptions: Open to all faiths and no faith; service is not contingent on religious participation. Undocumented residents are typically eligible.
eligibility.criteria_summary: At immediate risk of eviction or utility shutoff; income within program limit; can demonstrate ability to maintain rent after assistance.

resources.description: Monthly food benefits loaded onto an EBT card, usable at most grocery stores and many farmers markets. Benefit amount depends on household size, income, and certain expenses (rent, childcare, medical).

eligibility.exceptions: US citizens and certain qualified non-citizens (LPR with 5-year residency, refugees, asylees) are eligible. Children of immigrants may qualify even if parents do not. ABAWDs face time limits unless exempted.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Gross monthly income at or below 130 percent of federal poverty level; net at or below 100 percent; assets under $2,750 ($4,250 if elderly or disabled).

resources.description: Supplemental nutrition program for pregnant and postpartum people, infants, and children up to age 5. Provides specific healthy foods (milk, eggs, produce, infant formula), nutrition education, and breastfeeding support.

eligibility.exceptions: Income test waived if already enrolled in Medicaid, SNAP, or TANF. Open to all immigration statuses; use of WIC does not affect public charge determinations.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Pregnant, postpartum (up to 6 months), breastfeeding (up to 1 year), or have a child under 5; income at or below 185 percent FPL; nutritional risk assessment.

required_documents: Proof of income or program enrollment

resources.description: Nationwide network of food banks distributing groceries through partner pantries and direct distribution sites. Most pantries operate on a self-declaration basis with no proof-of-need required. Distribution frequency varies; many sites allow weekly visits.

eligibility.exceptions: Some pantries cap visit frequency per month; most accept walk-ins regardless of ID.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone with food insecurity; no income proof or ID typically required.

form.form_name: Food pantry visitor form (varies by site)

resources.description: Free or reduced-price breakfast and lunch in public schools. Applications are processed by the school district at the start of the year and any time circumstances change. Some districts now offer universal free meals.

eligibility.exceptions: Children in households receiving SNAP, TANF, or FDPIR are automatically eligible (direct certification). Foster, homeless, and migrant children are categorically eligible regardless of income.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Free meals at or below 130 percent FPL; reduced-price at 130-185 percent FPL; categorical eligibility for SNAP/TANF/foster/homeless/migrant children.

required_documents: Names and birthdates of all children

resources.description: Home-delivered meals for homebound seniors (typically 60+) and people with disabilities who cannot prepare meals safely. Drivers also do a brief wellness check. Sliding-scale fee or free; varies by local provider.

eligibility.exceptions: Many providers serve adults under 60 with disabilities. Veterans may qualify regardless of age via VA support.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Age 60 or older (some sites accept disabled adults under 60); homebound; unable to prepare meals or shop independently.

resources.address: Distributed via local food banks and pantries
resources.description: Federal commodity food distribution program operating through food banks and pantries. Provides shelf-stable foods (rice, beans, canned goods) plus dairy and produce.

eligibility.exceptions: Self-declaration of income at most sites; no proof required at distribution. Open to all immigration statuses.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Income at or below 130-200 percent FPL (varies by state). Residence in the state.

resources.address: Open sites at parks, libraries, schools, community centers
resources.description: Free meals and snacks for children 18 and under during summer school breaks. Open sites in low-income areas do not require enrollment or ID. Text FOOD to 304-304 to find the nearest site.

eligibility.exceptions: At open sites, any child 18 or under can walk up and eat. Closed-enrolled sites (camps) require registration.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Children 18 and under; meals served at open community sites during summer months.

form.form_name: No application; walk-up at open sites

resources.description: Coupons (typically $20-50 per season) for low-income seniors to buy fresh produce, herbs, and honey at farmers markets, roadside stands, and CSA programs.

eligibility.exceptions: Income limit is 185 percent FPL; varies by state. Distribution often first-come during summer.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Age 60 or older; income at or below 185 percent FPL.

resources.description: Volunteer-stocked, publicly accessible refrigerators offering free food 24/7 with no questions asked. Take what you need; leave what you can. Inventory varies by site and time of day.

eligibility.exceptions: No eligibility check whatsoever; mutual-aid model.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone in need can take food; no ID, registration, or income required.

resources.address: Local food banks and senior centers
resources.description: Monthly box of USDA commodity foods (cereal, canned fruits and vegetables, juice, pasta, peanut butter, milk, cheese) for low-income seniors. Box typically delivered or picked up at a partner site.

eligibility.exceptions: Income limit is 130 percent FPL. Open to all immigration statuses.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Age 60 or older; income at or below 130 percent FPL; residency in state.

resources.description: Joint federal-state public health insurance for low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and people with disabilities. Covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, mental health, long-term care, and more. Free or near-free.

eligibility.exceptions: Pregnant people and children of any immigration status qualify in many states. Emergency Medicaid covers emergency care for undocumented immigrants. Expansion states use 138 percent FPL; non-expansion states have lower limits.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Income at or below 138 percent FPL (expansion states) or lower; state residency; US citizen or qualified non-citizen (with exceptions for children and pregnancy).

form.form_name: Medicaid Application via state portal or Healthcare.gov
required_documents: Proof of citizenship or immigration status

resources.description: Low-cost or free comprehensive health coverage for children in families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance. Covers checkups, immunizations, dental, vision, and mental health.

eligibility.exceptions: Lawfully residing immigrant children qualify in 35+ states without the 5-year wait. Some states cover children regardless of status.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Children under 19; family income above Medicaid limit but at or below 200-400 percent FPL (varies by state); citizen or qualified immigrant.

required_documents: Proof of citizenship or immigration status

resources.agency_name: Federally Qualified Health Center Sliding-Scale Care
resources.description: Community health centers offering primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services to anyone, with fees on a sliding scale based on household income. Accept Medicaid, Medicare, private insurance, and self-pay.

eligibility.exceptions: Open to uninsured and undocumented patients. Sliding scale goes down to nominal fees (sometimes $0) at or below 100 percent FPL.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone needing primary care; uninsured and low-income patients pay reduced fees on a sliding scale.

required_documents: Proof of household income if requesting sliding scale

resources.address: Available by phone, text, and chat
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 phone, text, and chat support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Counselors can dispatch mobile crisis teams and connect callers to local mental health resources.

eligibility.exceptions: Press 1 for Veterans Crisis Line; press 3 for LGBTQI+ specialized support.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone in mental health crisis or supporting someone in crisis. No eligibility check.

resources.agency_name: SAMHSA National Helpline (Substance Use Treatment Referral)
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 information service that refers callers to local substance use disorder treatment, support groups, and community organizations. SAMHSA also offers an online treatment locator.

eligibility.exceptions: Helpline is free regardless of insurance; many referrals include sliding-scale and free programs.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone facing substance use issues or supporting someone who is. No eligibility check at the helpline.

resources.description: Federally funded comprehensive HIV care for low-income people living with HIV who are uninsured or underinsured. Covers antiretroviral therapy, primary care, mental health, dental, transportation, and case management.

eligibility.exceptions: Income limit varies by state (typically 400-500 percent FPL). Available regardless of immigration status. Pairs with AIDS Drug Assistance Program (ADAP) for medication coverage.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Documented HIV diagnosis; uninsured or underinsured; income at or below program limit; state residency.

resources.description: Nonprofit clearinghouse for patient assistance programs from pharmaceutical companies, plus discount cards, free clinics directory, and disease-specific assistance funds. Helps uninsured patients access expensive medications.

eligibility.exceptions: Each PAP has its own rules; NeedyMeds aggregates and helps users apply. Discount card is free and works at most pharmacies.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone needing help affording medications. Specific PAPs have their own income/insurance limits.

required_documents: Proof of income for specific PAPs

resources.address: Local free dental clinics and dental school clinics
resources.description: Network of free and reduced-cost dental clinics, dental school student clinics, and Donated Dental Services for adults who cannot afford care. Coverage varies; emergency extractions are often available even when routine cleanings are not.

eligibility.exceptions: Dental school clinics charge reduced fees and are open to all. Donated Dental Services serves elderly, disabled, and medically fragile patients.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Low-income adult without dental insurance; specific limits vary by clinic; some sites prioritize emergency cases.

form.form_name: Free dental clinic intake (varies by site)

resources.description: Free eye exam from a volunteer ophthalmologist for US seniors (65+) who have not seen an ophthalmologist in 3+ years and meet eligibility. Co-pays may apply for follow-up treatment, but the initial exam is free.

eligibility.exceptions: Must be legally residing in the US. No HMO insurance restriction.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Age 65 or older; US legal resident or citizen; no ophthalmologist visit in last 3 years.

resources.agency_name: Community Mental Health Center Sliding-Scale Therapy
resources.description: State and county-operated mental health centers providing counseling, psychiatric medication management, and crisis stabilization on a sliding-fee scale. Accept Medicaid; serve uninsured at reduced rates.

eligibility.exceptions: Some CMHCs prioritize severe and persistent mental illness; emergency walk-ins are usually accepted.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone needing mental health services; sliding-fee scale based on household income; reduced or no cost for low-income patients.

required_documents: Proof of income for sliding scale

resources.agency_name: Legal Services Corporation Civil Legal Aid
resources.description: Federally funded civil legal aid for low-income people on issues like eviction defense, family law, benefits appeals, consumer protection, and domestic violence. Free representation, advice, or self-help materials depending on the case.

eligibility.exceptions: Income limit is 125 percent FPL (200 percent for some asset cases). Certain immigrants face restrictions on LSC-funded representation, but exceptions exist for VAWA, T-visa, and U-visa applicants.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Income at or below 125 percent FPL; civil case in scope (no criminal defense, no fee-generating cases); state residency.

required_documents: All documents related to the legal issue

resources.description: Directory of nonprofit immigration legal service providers offering free or low-cost help with asylum, DACA, naturalization, family-based petitions, and removal defense. Searchable by state and service type.

eligibility.exceptions: Each provider has its own intake criteria; many prioritize detained individuals, asylum seekers, and unaccompanied children.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Anyone needing immigration legal help; intake limits and case-type focus vary by provider.

required_documents: Photo ID or any immigration document
All notices from USCIS or ICE

resources.address: Local courthouses and Public Defender offices
resources.description: Court-appointed criminal defense for indigent defendants who cannot afford a private attorney. Constitutionally guaranteed for any case carrying potential jail time. Coverage starts at arrest in many jurisdictions.

eligibility.exceptions: Income limits vary by jurisdiction. The court determines indigence; some defendants pay a partial application fee.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Criminal defendant unable to afford counsel; income screened by court; case carries potential incarceration.

resources.agency_name: Tenant Rights Hotline (National Housing Law Project)
resources.address: Phone-based; refers to local tenant advocates
resources.description: National advocacy organization with a referral hotline and self-help tenant rights information. For active eviction defense, refers callers to local legal aid; runs trainings for tenant-side advocates.

eligibility.exceptions: Self-help materials are free to all; direct representation is via local legal aid, not NHLP itself.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Any renter facing eviction, lease problems, or habitability issues; referral and self-help focused.

resources.address: Local DV legal advocates via hotline referral
resources.description: Confidential help getting protective orders, custody arrangements, immigration relief (VAWA, U-visa, T-visa), and divorce in the context of intimate-partner violence. Often free through legal aid or DV nonprofits.

eligibility.exceptions: Available regardless of gender, immigration status, or whether the survivor has left the relationship.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Survivor of intimate-partner violence needing civil legal help; no income or status check for the referral itself.

resources.agency_name: Disability Rights Network Protection and Advocacy
resources.description: Federally funded P&A organization in every state. Free legal advocacy on disability-related discrimination, special education, abuse and neglect in institutions, voting access, and benefits denials.

eligibility.exceptions: Each state P&A sets case priorities; many prioritize abuse and neglect investigations, education access, and employment discrimination.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Person with a disability needing advocacy or legal representation on disability-related issues; state residency.

required_documents: All correspondence related to the issue

resources.agency_name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Complaint System
resources.description: Federal complaint system for problems with banks, credit cards, mortgages, student loans, debt collectors, and credit reports. CFPB forwards complaints to the company and tracks responses; many disputes resolve within 15 days.

eligibility.exceptions: CFPB does not represent individuals in court; it pressures companies to respond and tracks systemic issues.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Any consumer with a complaint about a covered financial product or service.

required_documents: Copies of disputed bills or statements

resources.address: Local legal aid and law school clinics
resources.description: Help reviewing criminal records, filing petitions for expungement, record sealing, or set-aside in eligible cases. State-specific eligibility; some states have automatic clean-slate laws now.

eligibility.exceptions: Eligibility depends on offense type, time since conviction, and state law. Some convictions never expungeable; others may be sealed.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Adult with eligible past conviction; income-qualifying for legal aid; state-specific waiting period satisfied.

resources.description: American Bar Association online platform where qualifying users can post civil legal questions and get free written advice from a volunteer attorney within a few days. Covers most US states.

eligibility.exceptions: Income limit is 250 percent FPL. Civil matters only (no criminal cases). Cannot replace ongoing representation.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Adult with civil legal question; income at or below 250 percent FPL; resident of a participating state.

form.form_name: ABA Free Legal Answers question submission

resources.address: Accredited US law schools with clinics
resources.description: Law students supervised by clinical faculty provide free representation on a limited number of cases per semester, often in housing, immigration, family, or disability law. Cases are selected to fit the teaching curriculum.

eligibility.exceptions: Each clinic sets its own intake; cases are often selected for educational value as well as merit.
eligibility.criteria_summary: Low-income person whose case matches the clinic's practice area; intake windows align with academic semester.

required_documents: All documents related to the legal issue
